They are usually calculated as a percentage of the total value of thetransaction, but they can also be fixed amounts or based on other factors.Commissionfees can vary widely depending on the type oftransaction, the industry, themarketconditions, and the ...", "relevance_score": 0.6}, {"title": "What is \"take rate\" (or commission)? The take rate is also commonly referred to as \"rake\", \"commission,\" \"fee,\" or \"transactionfee\".", "relevance_score": 0.6}, {"title": "Affiliate Commission Statistics: Rates, Payouts & Benchmarks (2026)", "url": "https://wecantrack.com/insights/affiliate-commission-statistics/", "snippet": "[DDG Snippet] Affiliatecommissionstatisticsfor 2026. Comprehensive data on retailers, markets, products & consumers \u2013 worldwide and updated monthly. ## Featured Whitepapers Get Your Free Whitepaper Now [![Image 1: Consumer Shift the Marketplace Landscape](https://static.ecdb.com/media/2026/03/header-14210.png)](https://ecdb.com/whitepaper/consumer-shift-the-marketplace-landscape)[![Image 2: Global E-Commerce Compass 2026](https://static.ecdb.com/media/2026/02/slide1-14163.webp)](https://ecdb.com/whitepaper/global-e-commerce-compass-l)[![Image 3: E-Commerce Trends 2026](https://static.ecdb.com/media/2026/03/header-14204.png)](https://ecdb.com/whitepaper/ecommerce-trends)[![Image 4: E-Commerce in Europe 2026](https://static.ecdb.com/media/2026/03/header-14201.png)](https://ecdb.com/whitepaper/ecommerce-europe)[![Image 5: E-Commerce in North America 2026](https://static.ecdb.com/media/2026/03/header-14192.png)](https://ecdb.com/whitepaper/ecommerce-na)[![Image 6: E-Commerce in Germany 2026](https://static.ecdb.com/media/2026/03/header-14195.png)](https://ecdb.com/whitepaper/ecommerce", "relevance_score": 0.85}, {"title": "Statista - The Statistics Portal for Market Data, Market Research and ...", "url": "https://www.statista.com/", "snippet": "[Jina Parallel Content]  How crude oil prices are determined  As with most commodities, crude oil prices are impacted by supply and demand, as well as inventories and market sentiment. However, as oil is most often traded in future contracts (where a contract is agreed upon while product delivery will follow in the next two to three months), market speculation is one of the principal determinants for oil prices. Traders make conclusions on how production output and consumer demand will likely develop over the coming months, leaving room for uncertainty. [Spot prices](https://www.statista.com/statistics/209641/average-annual-spot-price-of-wti-and-brent-crude-oil/) differ from futures in so far as they reflect the current market price of a commodity. [Read more](https://www.statista.com/statistics/326017/weekly-crude-oil-prices/) ![Image 38](https://cdn.statcdn.com/Statistic/735000/735904-blank-355.png) Mar 10, 2026 | Semiconductors ### Share of Intel and AMD x86 computer CPUs worldwide 2012-2026, by quarter In the first quarter of 2026, roughly 60.1 percent of x86 computer processor or CPU tests recorded were from Intel processors, while 38.8 percent were AMD processors. When looking solely at laptop CPUs, Intel is the clear winner, accounting for over[70 percent of laptop CPU test benchmark results](https://www.statista.com/statistics/1130315/worldwide-x86-intel-amd-laptop-market-share/) in the first quarter of 2026.  Traditional x86 processors  The x86 is a family of instruction set architectures (ISA) for computer processors, otherwise known as central processing units (CPUs), which were initially developed by [Intel](https://www.statista.com/topics/1877/intel/). They refer to the way a CPU handles information, with x86 architecture of variable instruction lengths, and primarily of a complex instruction set computer (CISC) design. Although additions and developments to the x86 processor have taken place, backward compatibility has remained a key feature of the architecture; the newer x86 processors can run all the programs that older processors could run.  Apple Mac processors  The x86 processor is the [predominant hardware platform for laptops, desktops, and servers globally](https://www.statista.com/statistics/553450/worldwide-microprocessor-unit-sales-distribution-by-application/). Both Windows and Mac laptops have used x86 chips; previously, Apple\u2019s line of Mac products used PowerPC microprocessors, with the transition to Intel x86 architecture first acknowledged in 2005. The first generation of Intel-based Mac computers were released in January 2006, a partnership that continued through until 2020, where [Apple](https://www.statista.com/topics/847/apple/) announced the switch of the Mac line from Intel CPUs to Arm-based processors. The decision was made in order to standardize on one CPU architecture for all iPhone, iPad, and Mac products. [Read more](https://www.statista.com/statistics/735904/worldwide-x86-intel-amd-market-share/) ![Image 39](https://cdn.statcdn", "relevance_score": 0.85}, {"title": "What is marketplace commission (take rate?) - Sharetribe", "url": "https://www.sharetribe.com/marketplace-glossary/commission-take-rate/", "snippet": "[Jina Parallel Content] Title: What is marketplace commission (take rate?) URL Source: https://www.sharetribe.com/marketplace-glossary/commission-take-rate/ Markdown Content: ## Definition of commission (or take rate) Marketplace take rate or marketplace commission is the percentage of sales a marketplace charges for facilitating the transaction between a buyer and a seller. It can range from 0% to 50% or more, depending on the industry, type of sale, and geography, among other factors. Commissions represent the share earned by the marketplace for providing services such as platform usage, transaction processing, customer support, and marketing. Setting a balanced take rate lets [marketplace admins](https://www.sharetribe.com/marketplace-glossary/admin/) generate revenue while ensuring they provide more value for both buyers and sellers than what they charge. Your take rate directly influences various aspects of your business and growth strategy, including your scalability, user acquisition, and competitive positioning. Learn more marketplace lingo in our complete [marketplace glossary](https://www.sharetribe.com/marketplace-glossary/). ## Why commission is the most popular marketplace business model At Sharetribe, we've [studied how the best-performing global marketplaces make money](https://www.sharetribe.com/academy/how-top-100-marketplaces-monetize/). We found that charging a take rate/commission is the most popular among them. The reason why commission is so popular is that it works for all sides of the marketplace: the sellers, the buyers, and the marketplace owner. Users only pay you when they get value themselves. Before that, using the platform is free. There's low risk for them to join, and if your pricing strategy is well-planned (we'll discuss this later in this article), they receive more value from your marketplace than what they pay. Commission is usually the most lucrative business model for a marketplace founder. You get a piece of all the value -- GMV -- that passes through your marketplace. And as you grow, your commission profits do, too. For the same reason, take rate is also a very scalable business model. Finally, the flexibility in how a take rate is defined and which side is charged makes the model adaptable across niches and audience types. More on that next. ### Fixed vs. percentage-based take rates A marketplace can adopt two broad types of marketplace take rate models: fixed and percentage-based (also known as tiered). Some marketplaces use both.\n...\nTitle: What is marketplace commission (take rate?) URL Source: https://www.sharetribe.com/marketplace-glossary/commission-take-rate/ Markdown Content: ## Definition of commission (or take rate) Marketplace take rate or marketplace commission is the percentage of sales a marketplace charges for facilitating the transaction between a buyer and a seller. It can range from 0% to 50% or more, depending on the industry, type of sale, and geography, among other factors. Commissions represent the share ", "relevance_score": 0.85}]
